lines.text: Central Perk, Chandler and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading the comics) Eh..., I don't, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, as old as he is in dog years, do you think Snoopy should still be allowed to fly this thing?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Do you remember when you first came here, how you spent two weeks getting trained by another waitress?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, sure! Do you need me to train somebody new?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (laughs) Good one. Actually, ah, Terry wants you to take the training again, whenever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Chandler) Eh, do you believe that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (thinks about it) Yeah?
Opening Credits
The hallway of Ross's building, there is a Brown Bird girl selling cookies, as Ross and Chandler come up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: So that's two boxes of the Holiday Macaroons. On behalf of the Brown Birds of America, I salute you. (Does the Brown Bird salute, she blows on a bird call, then holds her hand, palm facing out, next to her face, and then waves it like a bird flapping it's wings.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just admit it Chandler, you have no backhand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse me little one, I have a very solid backhand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shielding your face and shrieking like a girl... is not a backhand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was shrieking... like a Marine.
they both start up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right here. Watch me execute the three 'P's of championship play. Power. (swings the racquet) Precision. (swings the racquet.) and penache. (does a backswing and hits Sarah who's started up the stairs, knocking her down, they both watch in horror.)
Central Perk, the gang's all there discussing the incident.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You broke a little girl's leg?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. I feel horrible. Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading the paper) Says here that a muppet got whacked on Seasame Street last night. (to Ross) Where exactly were around ten-ish?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I'm gonna go see her. I want to bring her something, what do you think she'll like?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe a Hello Kitty doll, the ability to walk...
Rachel starts to laugh, and Ross notices her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm gonna get back to retraining. (gets up)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, see you guys. (starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look out kids, he's coming! (Ross continues to leave with his head down in shame.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I gotta go sell some Christmas trees.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Have fun. Oh wait, no, don't! I forgot I am totally against that now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Me having a job?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, I am against innocent trees being cut down in their prime, and their, their corpses grotesquely dressed in like tinsel and twinkly lights. (to Joey) Hey, how do you sleep at night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm pretty tired from lugging the trees around all day. Hey, Phoebe listen, you got this all wrong. Those trees were born to be Christmas trees, their fulfilling their life purpose, by, by making people happy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?
Phoebe turns and looks at Monica, while Joey frantically motions to Chandler to help him out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Yes, and ah, ah, the trees are happy too, because for most of them, it's the only chance to see New York.
[cut to Gunther retraining Rachel.]

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: ...and after you've delivered the drinks, you take the empty tray....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gunther, Gunther, please, I've worked here for two and a half years, I know the empty trays go over there. (points to the counter.)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: What if you put them here. (sets the empty tray on another stack of empty trays on the back counter.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Well, y'know that's actually a really good idea, because that way they'll be closer to the mugs. Y'know what, you should have the other waitresses do that too.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: They already do. That's why they call it the 'tray spot.'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gee, I always heard them talk about that, I just always thought that it was a club they went to. Oh God, I'm, I'm sorry. (walks away)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: It's all right. Sweetheart.
Sarah's bedroom, her room is decorated with a space motif.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, this must be kinda neat for ya, huh? I mean, your Dad tells me that you get a couple of days off school, and you, you ah, don't have to sell those cookies anymore.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Well, I kinda wanted to sell the cookies. The girl who sells the most wins a trip to Spacecamp, and gets to sit in a real space shuttle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, you ah, you really like all this space stuff, huh?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Yeah. My Dad says if I spend as much time helping him clean apartments, as I do daydreaming about outer space, he'd be able to afford a trip to the Taj Mahal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think you would have to clean a whole lot of apartments to go all the way to India.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: No. The one in Atlantic City, Dad loves the slots. He says he's gonna double the college money my Grandma left me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh. Well, good luck to Dad. Say, how many more boxes would you have to sell in order to win?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: The girl who won last year sold four hundred and seventy-five.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: So far, I've sold seventy-five.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Four hundred, huh? Well, that sounds do-able. (starts to get out his wallet) How much are the boxes?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Five dollars a box.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (puts away his wallet) And what is second prize?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: A ten speed bike. But, I'd rather have something my Dad couldn't sell.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, that makes sense.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Could you do me one favor, if it's not too much trouble?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, Sarah, anything.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Could you pull open the curtains for me? The astronauts from the space shuttle are gonna be on the news, and since we don't have a TV, the lady across the alley said she'd push hers up to a window, so I could watch it.
A hallway, Ross is selling Brown Bird cookies for Sarah, he stops and knocks on a door.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (looking through her peephole, we see Ross standing in the hallway.) Yesss?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, I'm selling Brown Bird cookies.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: You're no Brown Bird, I can see you through my peephole.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, hi, I'm, I'm an honorary Brown Bird (does the Brown Bird salute.)

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, well, it means that I can sell cookies, but I'm not invited to sleep-overs.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I can dial 9-1-1 at the touch of a button, y'know. Now, go away!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, please, please, um, it's for a poor little girl who wants to go to Spacecamp more than anything in the world.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'm pressing, a policeman is on his way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay! I'm going. I'm going. (goes across the hall to knock on another door.)

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I can still see you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right!!
Joey's work, selling Christmas trees.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (walking up to Joey) Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. What, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I-I thought a lot about what you said, and um, I realilized duh, all right maybe I was a little judgmental. Yeah, (looks at the tree) oh, but oh...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look now, Phoebe remember, hey, their just fulfilling their Christmas....

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Destiny.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (One of Joey's co-workers, walks by with a dead tree.) Yikes! That one doesn't look very fulfilled.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that's, that's ah, one of the old ones, he's just taking it to the back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You keep the old ones in the back, that is so ageist.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well we have to make room for the fresh ones.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, what happens to the old guys?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, they go into the chipper.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why, do I have a feeling that's not as happy as it sounds? (Joey points out one going into the chipper to her, as this haunty, demonic music starts to play in the background) No! Nooooo!!! (she winces in horror and hides her face against Joey's shoulder, as she sees the tree spit out from the chipper.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the guy operating the chipper) Hey! Hey!! (makes the 'cut it' motion with his hands)
Central Perk, all except Phoebe are there, Ross is telling the gang, minus Rachel who's still being retrained, about the different cookie options.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...and these come in the shapes of your favourite Christmas characters, Santa, Rudolph, and Baby Jesus.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'll take a box of the cream filled Jesus's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, one box! Come on, I'm trying to send a little girl to Spacecamp, I'm putting you down for five boxes. Chandler, what about you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, do you have any coconut flavoured deities?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but ah, there's coconut in the Hanukkah Menoreoes. I tell you what, I'll put you down for eight boxes, one for each night.
Chandler mouths 'Okay.'

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I'll take one box of the mint treasures, just one, and that's it. I-I started gaining weight after I joined the Brown Birds. (to Ross) Remember, how Dad bought all my boxes and I ate them all?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, no Mon, Dad had to buy everyone of your boxes because you ate them all. But ah, y'know I'm sure that's not gonna happen this time, why don't I put you down for three of the mint treasures and just a couple of the Rudolph's.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on, now you know you want 'em.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't, don't, don't, don't, don't do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'll tell you what Mon, I'll give you the first box for free.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (she reaches out for it and stops) Oh God! I gotta go! (runs out)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! All the cool kids are eating 'em! (chases after her.)
[cut to Gunther retraining Rachel.]

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: And when you have a second later, I wanna show you why we don't just trap spiders under coffee mugs and leave them there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sitting down next to Chandler) I'm training to be better at a job that I hate, my life officially sucks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look Rach, wasn't this supposed to a temporary thing? I thought you wanted to do fashion stuff?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, yeah! I'm still pursuing that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How... exactly are you pursuing that? Y'know other than sending out resumes like what, two years ago?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'm also sending out.... good thoughts.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you ask me, as long as you got this job, you've got nothing pushing you to get another one. You need the fear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The fear?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's right, if you quit this job, you then have motivation to go after a job you really want.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then how come you're still at a job that you hate, I mean why don't you quit and get 'the fear'?
Chandler and Joey both laugh

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because, I'm too afraid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I mean I would give anything to work for a designer, y'know, or a buyer.... Oh, I just don't want to be 30 and still work here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, that'd be much worse than being 28, and still working here.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Remind me to review with you which pot is decaf and which is regular.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can't I just look at the handles on them?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: You would think.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, fine. Gunther, y'know what, I am a terrible waitress, do you know why I'm a terrible waitress? Because, I don't care. I don't care. I don't care which pot is regular and which pot is decaf, I don't care where the tray spot is, I just don't care, this is not what I want to do. So I don't think I should do it anymore. I'm gonna give you my weeks notice.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gunther, I quit.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) Does this mean we're gonna have to start paying for coffee? (Joey shrugs his shoulders.)
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is entering numbers on a calculator as Ross reads off how much he's sold.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ....and 12, 22, 18, four... (Chandler starts laughing) What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I spelled out boobies.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (comes up and starts looking through Ross's cookie supply) Ross, but me down for another box of the mint treasures, okay. Where, where are the mint treasures?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, we're out. I sold them all.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, I'm cutting you off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. No, just, just, just a couple more boxes. It-it-it's no big deal, all right, I'm-I'm cool. You gotta help me out with a couple more boxes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mon, look at yourself. You have cookie on your neck.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (covers her neck) Oh God! (runs to the bathroom)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, how many have you sold so far?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Check this out. Five hundred and seventeen boxes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God, how did you do that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, the other night I was leaving the museum just as 'Laser Floyd' was letting out of the planetarium, without even trying I sold 50 boxes! That's when it occurred to me, the key to my success, 'the munchies.' So I ah, started hitting the NYU dorms around midnight. I am selling cookies by the case. They call me: 'Cookie Dude!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Okay, stop what you're doing, I need envelope stuffers, I need stamp lickers.....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well hey, who did these resumes for ya?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me! On my computer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well you sure used a large font.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh, yeah, well ah, waitress at a coffee shop and cheer squad co-captain only took up so much room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey that's funny! Your funny Chandler! Your a funny guy! You wanna know what else is really funny?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Something else I might have said?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I don't know, weren't you the guy that told me to quit my job when I had absolutely nothing else to do. Ha! Ha! Ha! Ha! Ha!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie, calm down, it's gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's not gonna be okay Ross, tomorrow is my last day, and I don't have a lead. Okay, y'know what, I'm just gonna, I'm just gonna call Gunther and I'm gonna tell him, I'm not quitting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You-you-you don't wanna give into the fear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You and your stupid fear. I hate your fear. I would like to take you and your fear....

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, interrupting Rachel) Hey! I got great news!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Run, Joey! Run for your life! (runs out)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Rachel, listen, have you ever heard of Fortunata Fashions?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well my old man is doing a plumbing job down there and he heard they have an opening. So, you want me to see if I can get you an interview?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Yes, I would love that, oh, that is soo sweet, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not a problem.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And now for the great news.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, that wasn't the great news?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Only if you think it's better than this... (holds up an aerosol can) snow-in-a-can!! I got it at work. Mon, you want me to decorate the window, give it a kind of Christmas lookie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Christmas cookie?
Joey work, Joey is showing a guy a tree.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, and ah, this one here is a Douglas Fir, now it's a little more money, but you get a nicer smell.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Looks good. I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (running up carrying a tree) Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait! No, no, you don't want that one. No, you can have this cool brown one. (points to the almost dead tree she has)

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: It's-it's-it's almost dead!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay but that's why you have to buy it, so it can fulfil it's Christmas destiny, otherwise there gonna throw it into the chipper. Tell him, Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, the ah, trees that don't fulfil their Christmas destiny are thrown in the chipper.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I-I think I'm gonna look around a little bit more.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs, you gotta stop this, I working on commission here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey, guys. I'm here to pick out my Christmas tree.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well look no further, (shows her the dead one) this one's yours! Ahhh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is this the one that I threw out last year?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right y'know what, nevermind! Everyone wants to have a green one! I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I didn't mean to get so emotional, I guess it's just the holidays, it's hard.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh honey, is that 'cause your Mom died around Christmas?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I wasn't even thinking about that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. (turns and looks at Joey, who gives a 'way-to-go' thumbs up and smile.)
A Brown Bird meeting, Ross is there with the other Brown Birds to see who won the contest.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to the girl sitting next to him) Hi there. How many, how many ah, did you sell?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: I'm not gonna tell you! You're the bad man who broke Sarah's leg.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey now! That was an accident, okay.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: You're a big scrud.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's a scrud?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Why don't you look in the mirror, scrud.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't have too. I can just look at you.

lines.speaker: Leader
lines.text: All right girls, and man. Let's see your final tallies. (all the girls raise their hands) Ohhhh, Debbie, (looks at her form) 321 boxes of cookies, (to Debbie) Very nice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to himself) Not nice enough.

lines.speaker: Leader
lines.text: Charla, 278. Sorry, dear, but still good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to himself) Good for a scrud.

lines.speaker: Leader
lines.text: Oh, yes Elizabeth. Ah, 871.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's crap!! Sister Brown Bird. (to Elizabeth) Good going. (does the salute)

lines.speaker: Leader
lines.text: Who's next? (goes over and stands behind Ross, who's feverishly writing on his form, and clears her throat to get his attention.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi there!

lines.speaker: Leader
lines.text: Hi. And batting for Sarah, Ross Geller, 872. Although, it looks like you bought an awful lot of cookies yourself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, that is because my doctor says that I have a very serious.... nuget.... diffency.
Central Perk, Chandler, Phoebe, and Ross are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell us what happened, Brown Bird Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I lost. Some little girl loaned her uniform to her nineteen year old sister, who went down to the U.S.S. Nimitz, and sold over 2,000 boxes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Rachel, who's entering) Hey! How'd the interview go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I blew it. I wouldn't of even hired me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come here sweetie, listen, you're gonna go on like a thousand interviews before you get a job. (she glares at him) That's not how that was supposed to come out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is the worst Christmas ever.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what Rach, maybe you should just, y'know stay here at the coffee house.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't! It's too late! Terry already hired that girl over there. (points to her) Look at her, she's even got waitress experience. Last night she was teaching everybody how to make napkin.... (starts to cry) swans.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That word was swans.
The hallway between the two apartments, Chandler, Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel are coming up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well seeing that drunk Santa wet himself, really perked up my Christmas.
They start to go into Monica and Rachel's, their apartment is filled with all of the old Christmas trees from Joey's work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey and Monica
lines.text: (jumping up from behind the couch) Merry Christmas!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You saved them! You guys! Oh God, you're the best!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's like 'Night of the Living Dead Christmas Trees.'
phone rings

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (answering the phone) Hello? (listens) Yeah, this is she. (listens) Oh! You're kidding! You're kidding! (listens) Oh thank you! I love you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, everybody loves a kidder.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (hanging up the phone) I got the job!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: That's great! Hey! Excellent!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, God bless us, everyone.
Central Perk, Rachel is serving her last cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here we go. I'm serving my last cup of coffee. (the gang starts humming the graduation theme) There you go. (hands it to Chandler) Enjoy. (they all cheer)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross) Should I tell her I ordered tea?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, excuse me, everyone. Ah, this is my last night working here, and I ah, just wanted say that I made some really good friends working here, and ah, it's just time to move on. (at the counter Gunther starts to cry and runs into the back room) Ah, and no offence to everybody who ah, still works here, you have no idea how good it feels to say that as of this moment I will never have to make coffee again.
Rachel's new job, Rachel's boss is telling her what to do.

lines.speaker: Rachel's Boss
lines.text: Now Mr. Kaplan Sr. likes his coffee strong, so your gonna use two bags instead of one, see. Now pay attention, 'cause this part's tricky, see some people use filters just once.
Closing Credits
The hallway between the apartments, Ross is bringing Sarah to Joey and Chandler's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm, I'm sorry you didn't get to go to Spacecamp, and I'm hoping that maybe somehow, this may make up for it. Presenting Sarah Tuttle's Private Very Special Spacecamp!! (opens the door and Chandler and Joey jump up, their apartment is decorated like outer space, one of the leather chairs is covered in tinfoil.)

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Really Mr. Geller, you don't have to do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh come on! Here we go! (picks her up and puts her in the chair) Stand by for mission countdown!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (simulating an echo) Ten, ten.., nine, nine, nine...., eight, eight, eight... (Chandler hits him in the back of the head) Okay, Blast off!

They start shaking the chair likes it's flying into outer space. Ross picks up a soccer ball and starts spinning it in his hand and runs around the chair beeping like a satellite. Chandler also starts running around the chair and saying...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm an alien. I'm an alien.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no! An asteroid! (throws the soccer ball off the back of Joey's head.)
The camera zooms in on Sarah and she has a big smile on her face.
End
